observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatement.
1.7 We further recommended and both the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer agreed to reconcile their records to determine the
status and the correct balance of the CIP accounts.

2.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the misstatement.
2.7 Henceforth, record mobilization fees under the Advances to Contractors
account to ensure fairness of the financial statement presentation; and
2.8 We further recommended and both the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer agreed to collaborate and make sure that advances
are recouped through regular monitoring of project progress.

3.8 We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to complete the
physical inventory taking, render a report thereon and update the
Property Records based on the result of the inventory and reconcile with
the accounting records.
3.9 Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of property and
accounting records must be regularly undertaken and regarded with
utmost importance.

4.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to call the attention
of the employees who are still in service, to fully settle the amount
advanced as soon as possible. Otherwise, withhold their salaries until the
cash advances are fully settled; and
4.10 Henceforth, ensure that that no additional cash advance be granted to
any official or employee with pending unliquidated cash advance.

5.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the
records per general and subsidiary ledgers and the cashbook of the
previous Municipal Treasurer and prepare corresponding Journal Entry
Voucher to adjust those reconciling items affecting the books. Likewise,
inform the MT of the reconciling affecting the cashbook for proper
adjustment.
5.9 We also recommended and the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer agreed to continue to conduct regular reconciliation
of records at least every end of each quarter and any adjustment must be
corrected and resolved promptly.

6.11 We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator agreed to prepare a detailed AIP that sufficiently describes
all PPAs to be implemented, along with their corresponding costs. PPAs
must be defined with enough specificity so that all the Mayor needs to do
is execute the contract.
6.12 We also recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to
strictly adhere to Section 22(c) of RA No. 7160. This means ensuring that
obligations charged against appropriations described in generic terms
are supported by a separate authorization from the Sanggunian, distinct
from the general appropriation ordinance.
6.13 Lastly, we recommend and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require
the submission of a Sanggunian authorization as a supporting document
before allowing payments for contracts charged against lumpsum
appropriations.

7.6 We recommended and the management agreed to strictly adhere to the
provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 by ensuring that Purchase Requests
are prepared, and Purchase Orders are issued before the actual delivery
and consumption of FOL;
7.7 We further recommended and the management agreed to ensure that all
procurement transactions are properly documented with accurate dates
reflecting the actual sequence of events; and
7.8 Lastly, we recommended and the management agreed to direct the end-
user or the BAC, to submit the Purchase Orders within five (5) working
days from issuance thereof.

8.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer to conduct an extensive information campaign to
raise taxpayer awareness and promote participation in the tax amnesty
program under Section 30 of RA No. 12001, to assist the municipality in
reaching their collection target in subsequent years.

9.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and
maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and submit promptly in succeeding
years the complete set of journals and ledgers in printed and electronic
copies to the Office of the Auditor.

10.4 We recommended and the LSB agreed to strictly comply with the
submission requirements of quarterly SEF utilization reports to all
required agencies, as prescribed under existing laws and regulations, and
to prevent future lapses in transparency and accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with  the
Municipal Budget Officer,  agreed  to  prepare  the  Financial  Reports  and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or  before  February  14  of
the ensuing year pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular  No.  2010-001  dated
March 2, 2010.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to  create  an  Inventory
Committee to conduct complete annual physical inventory count  of  Property,
Plant and Equipment, and require the committee to prepare the Report on  the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and submit  a  copy  of  the
report to the COA Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

    Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of related PPE
records be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance.

We recommended and the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  prepare  and
immediately  submit  the  monthly  Bank  Reconciliation  Statements  of  all
depository accounts for CY 2021 to the Auditor, and  reconcile  her  records
with the records of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant  and  Municipal  Treasurer
agreed to timely reconcile records per cashbook as well as the  general  and
subsidiary ledgers before the latter foot and close the books at the end  of
each month.  We  also  recommended,  and  both  agreed  to  conduct  regular
reconciliation of records at  least  every  end  of  each  quarter  and  any
adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor thru  the  Municipal  Accountant
agreed to strictly require officials and  employees  who  have  unliquidated
cash advances to settle the amount advanced within  the  prescribed  period.
Otherwise, withhold their salaries until such time that  the  cash  advances
are fully settled.


    We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure  that
no additional cash advance be granted  to  any  official  or  employee  with
pending unliquidated cash advance.

We  recommended  and  the  Municipality  agreed   to   strengthen   its
information  dissemination  and  COVID-19  vaccination  campaign  to   fight
vaccine hesitancy and achieve the herd immunity set by DOH.

We recommended and the Municipal Health Officer agreed to  furnish  the
Municipal Accountant with the detailed list of allocated  vaccines  received
by  the  Municipality.  Furthermore,  we  recommended  that  the   Municipal
Accountant recognized in the books all the donations received in  accordance
with existing regulations.

    In case of donations received without  cost,  we  recommended  and  the
Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize the donations at their fair  market
value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained  by  reference  to
an active market or by appraisal as provided in paragraph 97  of  the  IPSAS
NO. 23.

We recommended  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  recognize
financial assistance as Trust Liability under General Fund  as  mandated  by
the guidelines set by the DBM.

      We also  recommended  and  the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  agreed  to
properly appropriate the fund through legislation  and  submit  Supplemental
Annual Investment Plan in compliance with existing regulations.

We recommended and the MSWD Officer agreed to  ensure  the  preparation
and  submission  of  the   Summary/List   of   Donations-in-Kind   Received,
Distributed and Balances to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnish  the  COA
Auditor, for appropriate recognition in the books of the  Municipality,  and
proper accounting, monitoring and validation of donations-in-kind.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the  Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal DRRM Officer to coordinate with each other  for
the preparation and submission of the Report on Sources and  Utilization  of
LDRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the  end
of each month.

We recommended and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  submit  the
required supporting documents for all the expenditures charged  against  20%
DF.

    We also recommended and the Municipal  Development  Council  agreed  to
ensure that only projects that partake the nature of investment  or  capital
expenditure are included in the 20% Annual Development Plan  to  attain  the
desirable socio-economic development in the Municipality.

We recommended and the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  require  the
Municipal Health Officer  to  submit  immediately  the  required  supporting
documents to establish the validity of SRA payment.

    We also recommended and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to  ensure
strict  compliance  with  the  prescribed  guidelines  before  granting  any
allowances to personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in  coordination
   with the Municipal Budget Officer,  prepare  the  Financial  Reports  and
   submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before February 14  of
   the ensuing year pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated
   March 2, 2010.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  and
    immediately submit to  the  Office  of  the  Auditor  the  monthly  Bank
    Reconciliation  Statements  of  all  depository  accounts  for  CY  2019
    pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74  of  PD  No.  1445for
    verification purposes and to validation of existence  of  cash  in  bank
    balances as presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain  from  utilizing
    casual employees to collect Municipal revenues and designate  only  duly
    bonded permanent employees in performing said function to  conform  with
    prescribed existing rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  strictly  require  the
    accountable officers in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to  comply
    with the bonding requirements and ensure that the fidelity bond  of  all
    accountable officers are renewed before expiration.

             Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discourage
    discharge of collecting functions of an accountable  personnel  that  is
    not properly bonded.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  thru  the  Municipal
   Accountant strictly require officials and employees who have unliquidated
   cash advances to settle the amount advanced within the prescribed periods
   pursuant  to  existing  regulations.   Otherwise,   recommend   for   the
   withholding of their salaries until such time that the cash advances  are
   fully settled or institute administrative or criminal case against erring
   employees.

We  recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  completes  its
   physical inventory  taking,  render  a  report  thereon  and  update  and
   reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that  the  PPE
   accounts totaling P43,388,257.63 are presented fairly in the Statement of
   Financial Position as of December 31, 2019.

            We further recommended that for all existing properties, as well
   as for subsequent procurements, the Municipal Treasurer, in his  capacity
   as Property Custodian, to prepare and maintain Property Cards  while  the
   Municipal Accountant prepare the PPE Ledger Card as basis  for  inventory
   taking

              Henceforth, periodic inventory taking  and  reconciliation  of
   related subsidiary records should be regularly  undertaken  and  regarded
   with utmost importance.

We recommended that the Municipality through its  LDRRM  Council  to
    review its LDRRM Plan, consolidate all unexpended  funds  from  previous
    years and formulate viable projects and activities to be implemented  in
    the ensuing year.  Relatively, the  LDDRM  Officer  ensures  the  timely
    utilization of the fund in order to improve the LGU's ability to prepare
    for and respond to threats of calamities and disasters.

We recommended that the LGU through the GAD  Focal  Person  submit
     the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) days from  end  of
     January of the preceding year together  with  the  required  supporting
     documents to strictly comply with the existing regulations.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer   submit   the
   corresponding disbursement vouchers and supporting documents  of  the  70
   checks issued totaling P2,031,702.47 to the  Municipal  Accountant  which
   shall be submitted to the Auditor for post audit in compliance to Section
   4 of Presidential Decree No.  1445.   We  further  recommended  that  the
   Municipal Treasurer  draw  checks  only  on  duly  approved  disbursement
   vouchers.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  Municipal
    Accountant  inventory  all  documents  in  their  offices  to  determine
    valueless records due for disposal based on the guidelines issued by the
    National Archives of the Philippines.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee completes its physical inventory taking, render a report thereon and update and reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that the PPE accounts totaling P42,373,277.88 are fairly presented in the balance sheet as of December 31, 2017. Henceforth, inventory taking and reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant had given information that inventory count will be done in CY 2018.
observations.reason: No report was still submitted for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board of the Municipality refrain from charging non-related expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses charged against SEF as provided under Section 272 of RA 7160 and Item 4 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017. We further recommended for the immediate settlement of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had requested the Municipal Accountant to review the documents already on file and to work out the settlement of the disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from charging expenses not related to GAD activities pursuant pertinent provisions to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We further recommended that the focal person shall be guided accordingly with the preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality of La Castellana and strictly adhere to the regulations of the aforementioned JMC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had commented that the GAD Focal Person will be guided accordingly in the future.
observations.reason: There are still expenses charged that were not directly related to GAD fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board of the Municipality refrain from including in appropriations as well as charging non-related expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses charged against SEF as provided under Section 272 of RA 7160 and Section 4 DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No 01 s 1998 dated April 14,1998.
observations.managementAction: For compliance.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should reconcile his cashbooks monthly and quarterly with the accounting records in compliance with the above-mentioned regulation to make sure that the balances appearing in his cashbooks are correct. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should verify the correctness of the Municipal Treasurer’s report and reconcile her GL/SL accounts with the cashbooks every end of the quarter to determine possible errors and effect appropriate adjustments, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: No action.
observations.reason: No reconciliati on had been made for balances between cash books and GLS as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in  coordination  with
   the Municipal Budget Officer, pursuant to Section 4 of COA  Circular  No.
   2010-001 dated March 2, 2010, prepare the Financial  Reports  and  submit
   the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before  February  14  of  the
   ensuing year.


        We  further  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require   the
   Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs,  payrolls  and  official  receipts
   within the prescribed  period  to  the  Municipal  Accountant  otherwise,
   impose acceptable sanctions, which include  withholding  of  salaries  of
   erring officials until compliance with requirements is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the  Municipal  Accountant
strictly  require  officials  and  employees  who  have  unliquidated   cash
advances to  settle  the  amount  advanced  within  the  prescribed  periods
pursuant to Section 5.1 of COA Circular No.  97-002.   Otherwise,  recommend
for the withholding of  their  salaries  pursuant  to  second  paragraph  of
Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002  until  such  time  that  the  cash
advances are fully settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination  with
the liaison officer of the Municipal Mayor, (1) submit to  the  Auditor  the
DVs pertaining to the cash advances granted  in  CY  2018  as  well  as  the
copy/ies of the transmittal letter/s, duly received by  the  ICFAU  for  the
liquidation of the cash advances and (2) submit to the  ICFAU  the  required
documents for the settlement of  cash  advances  for  confidential  expenses
within the prescribed period for proper recording  in  the  General  Ledgers
and presentation of correct balance of confidential expenses as of  year-end
in the Statement of Performance.

We  recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  completes  its
   physical inventory  taking,  render  a  report  thereon  and  update  and
   reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that  the  PPE
   accounts totaling P39,625,629.94 are presented fairly in the Statement of
   Financial Position as of December 31, 2018.


            We further recommended that for all existing properties, as well
   as for subsequent procurements, the Municipal Treasurer, in her  capacity
   as Property Custodian, to prepare and maintain Property Cards  while  the
   Municipal Accountant shall prepare the  PPE  Ledger  Card  as  basis  for
   inventory taking.


              Henceforth, periodic inventory taking  and  reconciliation  of
   related subsidiary records should be regularly  undertaken  and  regarded
   with utmost importance.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Development  Council  strictly
   adhere to the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1
   dated April 13, 2011 in the prioritization of projects to be included in
   the 20% Development  Fund.   Moreover,  the  Council  must  ensure  that
   projects to be funded  by  the  20%  DF  shall  partake  the  nature  of
   investment or capital expenditures and  included  among  the  enumerated
   projects under JMC 2011-1.


          We  also  recommended  that  funds   allocated   for   non-related
   expenditures be reprogrammed to other priority projects, activities  and
   programs identified in the Annual Development and Investment Plan.

We recommended that availability, utilization and balances of the
   LDRRMF for CY 2018 be properly computed and reported through  appropriate
   disclosure in the Notes to FS following the suggested format pursuant  to
   Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in  coordination
    with the  Municipal  Planning  and  Development  Officer  and  Municipal
    Engineer, properly review transactions pertaining to disbursements  from
    funds granted by NGAs; assign the correct  entries  and  record  correct
    amounts in the Registry of Public Infrastructure so as  to  present  the
    correct balance of Public Infrastructure account as of December 31, 2018
    and thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010, prepare the Financial Reports and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before February 14 of the ensuing year. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs, payrolls and official receipts within the prescribed period to the Municipal Accountant; otherwise, impose acceptable sanctions, which include withholding of salaries of erring officials until compliance with requirements is made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had reasoned out that the failure to prepare the reports on time were attributed to the delays in the submission of DVs from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The required reports were submitted way beyond deadline but was included in the AAR 2017. In CY 2018, the submission was still made beyond the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant strictly require officials and employees who have unliquidated cash advances to settle the amount advanced within the prescribed periods pursuant to 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Otherwise, recommend for the withholding of their salaries pursuant to second paragraph of Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 until such time that the cash advances are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had sent demand letters to accountable officers requesting settlement of cash advances. Requirement to settle outstanding cash advances shall be followed thru in CY 2018. Further, the Accountant will prepare and submit an updated schedule of cash advances to disclose settlements on the December 31, 2017 balances.
observations.reason: Only some of prior year’s advances were settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the liaison officer of the Municipal Mayor, (1) submit to the Auditor the DVs pertaining to the cash advances granted in CY 2017 as well as the copy/ies of the transmittal letter/s, duly received by the ICFAU for the liquidation of the cash advances and (2) submit to the ICFAU the required documents for the settlement of cash advances for confidential expenses within the prescribed period for proper recording in the General Ledgers and presentation of correct balance of confidential expenses as of year-end in the Statement of Performance.

The Municipal Accountant had affirmed compliance with the recommendation in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Transactions in CY 2017 were submitted beyond the prescribed period. For CY 2018, claims were still submitted beyond the deadline and were not completely documented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the unexpended amount of P2,995,874.95 of LDRRMF for CY 2017 shall be recorded in the Trust Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002. A copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the said transfer with the updated balance should be submitted to the Auditor as proof of compliance.
observations.managementAction: Transfer and recording in the Trust Fund books was done in CY 2018. Reminder to the Municipal Accountant was given that the recording of the unexpended portion of LDRRMF shall be included in the year-end adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that availability, utilization and balances of the LDRRMF for CY 2017 be properly computed and reported through appropriate disclosure in the Notes to FS following the suggested format pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had prepared the utilization report for DRRMF showing utilization and balances at year-end.
observations.reason: The Notes to FS did not disclose in detail utilizations made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Budget Officer allocate a budget equivalent to 20% of total IRA to finance programs and projects for local development in compliance with Section 287 of Republic Act No. 7160, the Local Government Code. Accordingly, we recommended that appropriation for LCPC shall be provided separately.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer had provided a separate appropriation for CY 2019 only.
observations.reason: Separate provision from 20% Development Fund for LCPC will be complied in CY 2019 budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the seller (Angela Estate) to immediately process for the issuance of a TCT in the name of the Municipality for the lot purchased so as to assure ownership and prevent exposure of government property to possible loss.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had informed that the transfer including the preparation of the TCT is still on process. She will require for annotation of the sale made to the Municipality in the mother title for the time being.
observations.reason: No document had been submitted to show annotation in the mother title or the new title in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in the implementation of projects funded by BUB so that the targeted beneficiaries could avail of the benefits from the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had assured the immediate implementation of projects funded by the BUB. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, will submit an updated report on the status of implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Project in Charge of all BUB projects prepare utilization report/s, status of project implementation, and consequently return fund balances of all completed projects pursuant to the provisions in the MOA. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant prepare the subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfer, as to the source and the name of the project and disclose the same in the consolidated Notes to FS at year end.
observations.managementAction: A report is being prepared monthly to monitor the status of each project. Unutilized balance after implementation were refunded. A new personnel was assigned in accounting and was tasked to prepare the subsidiary ledgers. Disclosure will be done on the Notes to FS at year end.
observations.reason: In CY 2018, no JEV or other documents had been submitted for action taken on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Councils strictly adhere to the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 in the prioritization of projects to be included in the 20% Development Fund. Moreover, the council must ensure that projects to be funded by the 20% DF shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and included among the enumerated projects under JMC 2011-1. Also, it was recommended that funds allocated for non-related expenditures including unobiigated for both current and continuing appropriations, be reprogrammed to other priority projects activities and programs identified in the Annual Development and Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: For compliance, Proper implementation of the 20% Development Fund was discussed with the MPDO during the exit conference. Unexpended/unutilized balances of continuing appropriations are reprogrammed to other priority projects.
observations.reason: There were programs that still do not partake the nature of investment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality thru its Solid Waste Management Board prepare plans for safe closure and rehabilitation of the existing controlled dumpsite and its eventual conversation to a sanitary landfill to conform to provisions of RA 9003. We also recommended the conversion of the Municipality’s controlled dumpsite into an Eco-Park with Residual Containment Area (RCA) or sanitary landfill facility pursuant to Section 16 of R.A. 9003. We also recommended that the Municipality strengthen their Information and Education Campaign (IEC) by involving various schools and nearby communities.
observations.managementAction: A 10-year plan was drafted and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.
observations.reason: The Municipality still maintains a controlled dump site due to lack of funds to construct a sanitary land fill.

It is reminded that any payments in excess of services already rendered shall be the responsibility of the erring official.

observations.managementAction: Due to disallowance of this amount, partial refund had been done by employees thru salary deduction. Extension had been requested for the settlement of the ND.
observations.reason: The amount disallowed had not been fully settled. Withholding on salaries of all accountable officers is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the required documents for verification in order to prevent said transactions from possible suspension or disallowance. We further recommended that the municipal accountant should examine and check the correctness of all supporting documents before recording the transactions in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted some of the required documents for verification.
observations.reason: Continuous coordination with the concerned departments is still ongoing to facilitate the submission of other required documents.

Similar transactions shall be recorded in the same manner. observations.managementAction: The department in charge of the monitoring and evaluation of the projects was already informed to submit a status report as of December 31, 2011 for thorough review.
observations.reason: No report had been submitted for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that cash advances granted for salaries and wages shall be supported with duly approved payrolls. We recommend that the disbursing officer accomplish the Reports of Disbursement (ROD) for accounting of all cash advances and submit these reports with the paid payrolls and other supporting documents within five (5) working days from each 15th or end of month to the accounting office as liquidation and the basis for recording in the CDI.
observations.managementAction: For compliance. RODs were already prepared upon liquidation; however, it was noted that no payrolls were attached to cash advances granted for salaries and allowances. Cash advances were settled before the granting of a new cash advance.
observations.reason: RODs were already prepared upon liquidation; however, it was noted that no payrolls were attached to cash advances granted for salaries and allowances. Cash advances were settled before the granting of a new cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant comply with the cited provision of law. The Accountant should monitor its liabilities to barangays for these shares and draw vouchers for its payments quarterly. The Municipal Treasurer should also earmark cash allocation for the payments/remittances.
observations.managementAction: Remittances of Barangay share is done on quarterly basis.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, no schedule had been prepared in support of Due to LGUs account.

   pursuant to 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.  Otherwise, recommend for the
   withholding of their salaries pursuant to  second  paragraph  of  Section
   5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 until such time that the  cash  advances
   are fully settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination  with
the liaison officer of the Municipal Mayor, (1) submit to  the  Auditor  the
DVs pertaining to the cash advances granted  in  CY  2017  as  well  as  the
copy/ies of the transmittal letter/s, duly received by  the  ICFAU  for  the
liquidation of the cash advances and (2) submit to the  ICFAU  the  required
documents for the settlement of  cash  advances  for  confidential  expenses
within the prescribed period for proper recording  in  the  General  Ledgers
and presentation of correct balance of confidential expenses as of  year-end
in the Statement of Performance.

We recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  completes  its
   physical inventory  taking,  render  a  report  thereon  and  update  and
   reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that  the  PPE
   accounts totaling P42,373,277.88 are  fairly  presented  in  the  balance
   sheet as of December 31, 2017.


             Henceforth, inventory  taking  and  reconciliation  of  related
   subsidiary records should  be  regularly  undertaken  and  regarded  with
   utmost importance.

We recommended that the unexpended amount of P2,995,874.95 of LDRRMF
   for CY 2017 shall be recorded in the Trust Fund books in accordance with
   COA Circular No. 2012-002.  A copy of the Journal  Entry  Voucher  (JEV)
   for the said transfer with the updated balance should  be  submitted  to
   the Auditor as proof of compliance.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  and  Municipal  Budget
   Officer allocate a budget equivalent to  20%  of total  IRA  to  finance
   programs and projects  for  local  development  in  compliance  with  the
   Section 287 of the Local Government Code.   Accordingly,  we  recommended
   that appropriation for LCPC shall be provided separately.

We recommended that the Local School Board of  the  Municipality
   refrain from charging non-related expenses to the Special Education  Fund
   and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses
   charged against SEF as provided under  Section 272 of RA 7160 and Item 4
   of DepEd-DBM-DILG  Joint  Circular  No.  01  s.  2017.   We  further
   recommended  for   the   immediate   settlement   of   disallowance.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the seller  (Angela
   Estate) to immediately process for the issuance of a TCT in the name  of
   the Municipality for the lot purchased so as  to  assure  ownership  and
   prevent exposure of government property to possible loss.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit promptly the reports of collections and disbursement vouchers within the mandatory period to the Accounting Office to facilitate the preparation of the required year-end financial reports and supporting schedules and cause the timely submission of said reports in strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the submission of monthly reports of collections and copies of disbursement vouchers and apply appropriate measures for facilitation of submission of the same documents to the Accounting Office. Subsequently, information required for the timely preparation of Financial Statements shall be gathered from other Offices including the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer as budget and actual implementation form part and parcel of year-end financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that there were still delays in the submission of collection reports from the MTO despite periodic memoranda issued to the effect.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still delayed in the preparation and submission of financial reports for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant ensures completeness of the required documents before processing and releasing fund transfers to NGOs/POs as well as the requirement of submission of the final fund utilization report pertaining to the fund transferred in compliance with pertinent provisions of COA rules and regulations. We further recommended that the concerned official furnish this Office copies of the documents as required.
observations.managementAction: Some documents had been submitted for review.
observations.reason: The Municipality had requested for reasonable period to complete the documentary requirements. The amount had been suspended in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality thru the Municipal Accountant reclassify disbursements amounting to P320,000.00 that were initially recorded as outright expenses to Other Receivables (Loans) and provide the necessary subsidiary ledger per beneficiary. Similar transactions shall be recorded in the same manner. We further recommend that full cost recovery shall only be recorded upon submission of a Final Utilization Report. We further recommend for formulation of a repayment scheme and close monitoring of cost recovery of this project so as to intensify self-sustainability and avoid wastage of government funds. As mandated under Section 476 (4) of Republic Act No. 7160, the Local Government Code of 1991, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) shall monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects, activities in the local government units concerned in accordance with the approved development plan and (6) analyze the income and expenditure patterns, and formulate and recommend fiscal plans and policies for the consideration of the finance committee of the LGU concerned.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials had been informed of the recommendation.
observations.reason: In CY 2015, the LGU continued to extend financial assistance to similar associations without recording transactions as receivables.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer should reconcile his cashbooks monthly and quarterly with the accounting records in compliance with the above-mentioned regulation to make sure that the balances appearing in his cashbooks are correct. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should verify the correctness of the Municipal Treasurer’s report and reconcile her GL/SL accounts with the cashbooks every end of the quarter to determine possible errors and effect appropriate adjustments, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: No action.
observations.reason: No reconciliation had been made for balances between cash books and GLs as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance by concerned accountable officers be made to preclude implementation of sanctions as embodied in the circular.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat was informed. Schedule of submission of copies of POs as well as the supporting documents was decided upon.
observations.reason: No copies of contracts and purchase orders including the acceptance and inspection reports were submitted to the Office of the Auditor for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Project  In-
    charge of all BuB projects to  prepare  utilization  report,  status  of
    project implementation, and consequently return  fund  balances  of  all
    completed projects pursuant to the provisions in the MOA.

             Moreover,  the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  the  subsidiary
    ledgers for each fund transfer, as to Source and name  of  project,  and
    disclose the same in the consolidated Notes to FS at year-end.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Development  Council  strictly
   adhere to the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1
   dated           April 13, 2011 in the prioritization of projects  to  be
   included in the 20% Development Fund.  Moreover, the Council must ensure
   that projects to be funded by the 20% DF shall  partake  the  nature  of
   investment or capital expenditures and  included  among  the  enumerated
   projects under JMC 2011-1.


          We  also  recommended  that  funds   allocated   for   non-related
   expenditures including  unobligated  for  both  current  and  continuing
   appropriations, be reprogrammed to other priority  projects,  activities
   and programs identified in the Annual Development and Investment Plan.

We recommended that  the  Municipality  thru  its  Solid  Waste
   Management Board prepare plans for safe closure and rehabilitation of the
   existing controlled dumpsite and its eventual conversion  to  a  sanitary
   landfill to conform to provisions of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Local School  Board  of  the  Municipality
   refrain from including in appropriations as well as charging  non-related
   expenses to the  Special  Education  Fund  and  strictly  adhere  to  the
   regulations on the prioritization of  expenses  charged  against  SEF  as
   provided under Section 272 of RA 7160  and  Section  4  of  DECS-DBM-DILG
   Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal   Mayor   instructs   the
   BAC/Inspectorate  Committee  to  furnish  the  Audit   Team   copies   of
   perfected/issued contracts and purchase/job orders and  their  supporting
   documents as  well  as  inspection  and  acceptance  reports  within  the
   prescribed period mandated by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001  dated  February
   12, 2009.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal  Government,
   thru the GAD focal person, immediately submit  the  required  reports  as
   prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum  Circular  (JMC)  2013-1
   for further evaluation.  A format  was  already  provided  to  facilitate
   submission.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipality  thru  the  Municipal
   Accountant reclassify disbursements amounting to  P130,000.00  that  were
   initially recorded as outright expenses to Due from Other LGU (account 1-
   03-03-030) and provide the necessary subsidiary ledger  per  beneficiary,
   where applicable.  Similar transactions should be recorded  in  the  same
   manner.  We further recommended that expenses must only be recorded  upon
   submission of  Liquidation  Report  for  the  financial  assistance  duly
   supported with appropriate documents.

We  recommended  that  concerned  officials  ensure  that   all
   insurable properties of the Municipality are  insured  with  the  General
   Insurance Fund of the GSIS to protect the same against loss or damage and
   in case of loss or destruction, the Municipality can at least recover the
   costs of these assets.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  monitor  the  monthly
   preparation and  submission  of  the  Financial  Statements  pursuant  to
   Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.


        We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the  Report
   of Checks issued and Report of Disbursements daily for  each  fund  which
   shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for preparation of JEV and
   Journals pursuant to Section 43 and 46 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  appoint  or  assign  a
   permanent personnel in the Accounting Office to assist in the preparation
   of the bank reconciliation statements for all depository  banks  so  that
   the reports could be submitted to all concerned offices on time  pursuant
   to COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of PD 1445 and also  to  assure
   that cash  in  bank  balances  are  presented  fairly  in  the  financial
   statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere to
   Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No.  97-002  dated  February  10,  1997  by
   ensuring that no additional cash advances shall be granted to accountable
   officers who have not yet settled their previous advances.


              It was  further  recommended  that  all  Accountable  Officers
   immediately  submit  Liquidation  Reports  to   the   Accounting   Office
   especially if the purpose of the amount advanced had already been served.

We  recommended  that  the   Municipal   Accountant   submit   the
   Disbursement Vouchers pertaining to cash  advances  granted  in  CY  2016
   charged to Confidential/Intelligence pursuant to Item 6.1.8.4 of  JC  No.
   2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.


         She must also submit copy/ies of the  transmittal  letter/s,  duly
   received by the ICFAU for the liquidation of cash  advances  pursuant  to
   Item 6.2.2.4 pursuant to JC No. 2015-01.   Further,  we  recommended  the
   recording of expenses under Confidential Expenses  (account  5-02-10-010)
   as applicable to LGUs for succeeding years.  Emphasis added.

We recommended  that  the  Inventory  Committee  completes  its
   physical inventory  taking,  render  a  report  thereon  and  update  and
   reconcile the related subsidiary records to have assurance that  the  PPE
   accounts totaling P42,918,556.84 are presented fairly in the Statement of
   Financial Position as of December 31, 2016.


             We further recommend that for all existing properties, as  well
   as for subsequent procurements, the Municipal Treasurer, in his  capacity
   as  Property  Custodian,  prepare  Property  Cards  while  the  Municipal
   Accountant shall prepare the PPE  Ledger  Card  as  basis  for  inventory
   taking


              Henceforth, periodic inventory taking  and  reconciliation  of
   related subsidiary records should be regularly  undertaken  and  regarded
   with utmost importance.

We recommended that the remaining PDAF of P605,902.00 be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund (GF) pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 applying the prescribed rules on fund transfer from Trust Fund, where these remaining balances are booked, to the GF. Subsequently, these funds should be remitted or returned to the DOF thru the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Review was made on the outstanding balance and subsequently remitted to concerned office.
observations.reason: No information on the disposition of the outstanding balance as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality thru the Municipal Accountant reclassify disbursements amounting to P100,000.00 that were initially recorded as outright expense to Due from LGUs. Similar transactions should be recorded in the same manner. We further recommended the documents required for release of grant to the league be attached to the claim. Settlement for the fund transfer should only be recorded upon submission of completely documented final Fund Utilization Report.
observations.managementAction: Amount shall be reclassifie d and documents shall be required for the release of grant and subsequent settlement.
observations.reason: No JEV had been submitted giving information for the reclassification of disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the unexpended amount of approximately P10,983,699.84 (balance as of December 15, 2015) of LDRRMF should be transferred from General Fund books to Trust Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002. A copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the said transfer with the updated balance should be submitted to the Auditor as proof of compliance. We further recommended the prompt preparation of the Report and Utilization of DRRMF as well as its immediate submission within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Transfer was to be made in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Amount of P11+M for CY 2011-2015 was transferred per Notes to FS. No transfer had been made of the unutilized balance for CY 2016 of P4.2M per Report on Receipt and Utilization of DRRMF as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that availability, utilization and balances of the LDRRMF for CY 2015 be properly computed and reported through appropriate disclosure in the Notes to FS following the suggested format pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Concerned official that information will be disclosed in future Notes.
observations.reason: Information only of the transfer of balances for CY 2011-2015 of P11+M had been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal government implement the minimum budgetary requirement for Gender and Development of 5% of the total budget for the succeeding year in compliance to Section 33 of the General Provisions of GAA FY 2014 as well as pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The personnel concerned was required to prepare and submit the said reports.
observations.reason: No GAD Accomplishment Report had been submitted for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local school board of Municipality of La Castellana refrain from charging non-related expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly adhere to the regulations on the Prioritization of Expenses charged against SEF as provided under Section 272 of RA 7160 and Section 4 of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Officials assured that proper charges will be made to SEF.
observations.reason: Disbursements not included were still charged but was minimized in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the Municipal Development Council shall exercise prudence in formulating programs and projects for appropriation including the proposed disbursements in the 20% Development Fund and should be guided accordingly by Section 2.3 of DILG– DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 that all charges should be directly related to an investment or a capital expenditure.
observations.managementAction: LCE had agreed to review charges so as assured of capital expenditure of said charges.
observations.reason: There were still charges that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly examine projects in the continuing appropriations at year-end, especially those that remained unobligated. In coordination with the Municipal Budget Office, representation to the Local Chief Executive should be made that appropriations no longer needed will be recommended for immediate reversion to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for appropriation to programs and projects that conform with the guidelines set in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer will evaluate projects in the continuing appropriations and effect measures, for compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and immediately submit to the office of the Auditor the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository accounts for Cy 2015, for verification, pursuant to COA Circ. No. 96-011 and Section 74 of PD 1445 to assure that cash in bank balances are presented fairly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that she had started on the preparation of BRS in CY 2016.
observations.reason: No BRS had been submitted for all depository accounts of the LGU for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher/s to correct the balance of PCF. Recommended entry to correct replenishments of petty cash fund for CY 2012 until 2014 is as follows: Prior Years’ Adjustments  xx Petty Cash Fund xx
observations.managementAction: Adjustment was made in the ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enforce strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Cash advances amounting to P19,291,124.72 should be fully liquidated at the end of the year to protect government funds from possible losses. Consequently, this will redound to the presentation of correct balances of the cash and government equity accounts at year end.
observations.managementAction: Notices had been distributed to accountable officers and employees.
observations.reason: Cash advance balances for all funds had increased as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that concerned officials thoroughly review the claims made to the fund and properly record to the control job sheets as to be assured of the correctness of the amounts charged. It is reminded that any payments in excess of services already rendered shall be the responsibility of the erring official.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials had agreed to coordinate records to comply with the observation.
observations.reason: Payment for implemented projects had been monitored. However, no control job sheets had been presented for CY 2016 projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC, Secretariat and TWG personnel immediately submit the required documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001.  We further request the submission of the correct computation for the honorarium due for each BAC, Secretariat and TWG members.
observations.managementAction: BAC members were informed of the observation.
observations.reason: Partial requirements were attached to claims by BAC. Other documents were kept on file.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan be reviewed and updated as required under Section 16 of RA 9003 for the purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management. Also, strengthen the Barangay SWM Committee and fast track the creation for newly – elected barangay officials. We also recommend the conversion of the Municipality’s controlled dumpsite into an Eco-Park with Residual Containment Area (RCA) or sanitary landfill facility pursuant to Section 16 of R.A. 9003. We also recommend that the Municipality strengthen their Information and Education Campaign (IEC) by involving various schools and nearby communities. We also recommend the integration of SWM orientation upon renewal / application of mayor’s permit and business licenses as part of their strategies to ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management program in the entire municipality.
observations.managementAction: A 10-year plan was drafted and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.
observations.reason: No report had been submitted for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer speeds up the preparation of his report and submit the same to the accountant without delay so that the transactions could be recorded on time. The Municipal Treasurer/ Mayor should seriously look into the reasons why reports are delayed and employ corrective measures to cure this reporting deficiency based on the reasons uncovered.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal Treasurer has delegated some of the tasks to his subordinates to speed up the preparation of reports.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, transactions were still not immediately recorded in the cash book.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the regulation of not granting new cash advance to the Accountable Officers with previous unsettled cash advance. We also recommend that management shall send written notice to concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances for settlement, especially those long outstanding as of December 31, 2011 in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to accountable officers and partial liquidation had been made.
observations.reason: Additional cash advances are still granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the corresponding disbursement vouchers together with the related supporting documents pertaining to the 69 checks totaling P2,016,344.96. Henceforth, they must see to it that disbursement vouchers corresponding to the report of checks issued are complete and intact with the required supporting documents upon submission to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the DVs for evaluation. Accountable officers had been informed of this deficiency.
observations.reason: There were still DVs that were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management shall comply with the existing regulation in the submission of financial reports within the prescribed period so that it could be used as guide and tool by the management in making wise decisions and future plan for the development of the LGU as well as benefits for its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Transactions for January to November 2014 were submitted to Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The submission of DVs from Municipal Treasurer’s Office to the Accounting Office were still delayed that the preparation of monthly financial statements were not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend compliance with the following: Create a Committee for the inspection and appraisal of the unserviceable properties and prepare report. The Municipal Treasurer/ Property Custodian shall, through the Local Chief Executive, file an application for disposal of the unserviceable properties with the COA Audit Team. The Municipal Treasurer/ Property Custodian shall coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for proper classification of the unserviceable items to Other Asset Account.
observations.managementAction: Inspection, appraisal and disposal committees had been created. Request for inspection of properties for disposal had been submitted.
observations.reason: No JEVs had been submitted for CY 2016 signifying reclassification of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality of La Castellana submit a copy of the Obligation Request (ObR) covering DV No. 12-12-3006 totaling P4,236,500.00 to ascertain the existence of available appropriation of the above stated disbursement pursuant to Section 37, Volume I of Manual of the New Government Accounting System and COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated Jan 31, 2006.
observations.managementAction: Due to disallowance of this amount, partial refund had been done by employees thru salary deduction. Extension had been requested for the settlement of the ND.
observations.reason: The amount disallowed had not been fully settled. Withholding on salaries of all accountable officers is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the required documents for verification in order to prevent said transactions from possible suspension or disallowance. We further recommend that the municipal accountant should examine and check the correctness of all supporting documents before recording the transactions in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already submitted some of the required documents and Accountable officers had been informed of this deficiency.
observations.reason: Continuous coordination with the concerned departments is still ongoing to facilitate the submission of other required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality thru the Municipal Accountant reclassify disbursements amounting to P1,330,500.00 that were initially recorded as outright expenses to Other Receivables (Loans) and provide the necessary subsidiary ledger per beneficiary. observations.reason: No status report had been submitted for CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that cash advances granted for salaries and wages shall be supported with duly approved payrolls. We recommend that the disbursing officer accomplish the Reports of Disbursement (ROD) for accounting of all cash advances and submit these reports with the paid payrolls and other supporting documents within five (5) working days from each 15th or end of month the accounting Office as liquidation and the basis for recording in the CDI.
observations.managementAction: RODs were already prepared upon liquidation; however it was noted that no payrolls were attached to cash advances granted for salaries and allowances.
observations.reason: RODs were already prepared upon liquidation; however it was noted that no payrolls were attached to cash advances granted for salaries and allowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant comply with the cited provision of law. The Accountant should monitor its liabilities to barangays for these shares and draw vouchers for its payments quarterly. The Municipal Treasurer should also earmark cash allocation for the payments/remittances.
observations.managementAction: Remittances of Barangay share is done on quarterly basis.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, no schedule had been prepared in support of Due to LGUs account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency create specific criteria and implementing guidelines in the selection of scholars as provided in Article 100(xi) of the rules and regulations implementing the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines for scholarship were approved.
observations.reason: No copies of these guidelines were furnished to our Office for reference.
